question: In C programming, what keyword is used to define a structure?
explanation: The 'struct' keyword is used to define a structure, which allows grouping variables of different types under a single name.

question: What is a key difference between a structure and a union?
options: A structure allocates memory for all its members simultaneously, while a union allocates memory for one member at a time.
A union allocates memory for all its members simultaneously, while a structure allocates memory for one member at a time.
Structures can only hold data of the same type, while unions can hold different types.
Unions use less memory but are slower to access than structures.
explanation: A structure's total size is the sum of its members' sizes (plus padding), as all members have their own memory. A union's size is that of its largest member, as all members share the same memory block.
The integer value 65 is stored in the union's memory. Even though this memory can also be interpreted as a char (which would be 'A'), the value stored is still the integer 65 until it is overwritten.

question: Why must you be cautious when accessing a union member that was not the last one written to?
options: It leads to undefined behavior because you are interpreting the same memory bits as a different data type.
It will always cause a segmentation fault.
The compiler will automatically convert the value to the correct type.
It is perfectly safe and the value will be converted implicitly.
explanation: Unions do not store type information. Accessing a member that wasn't the last one written to means you are re-interpreting the raw binary data in memory as a different type, which is implementation-defined and often leads to bugs.

question: What is the primary purpose of using a union within a structure that includes a 'tag' field (a.k.a. a tagged union)?
options: To create a variant record that can safely handle different data types by explicitly tracking which member is currently valid.
To reduce the overall memory footprint of the structure by compressing its data.
To allow the structure to inherit properties from multiple different types simultaneously.
To improve performance by allowing faster access to the union's members.
explanation: A tagged union uses an additional enum or integer member (the tag) to explicitly record which member of the union was most recently written to. This allows a program to check the tag before accessing a union member, preventing the undefined behavior of accessing the wrong member.
